---
title: "Sentences"
---

::: {.callout-tip}
# Learning Goals: Sentences
1. Be able to define the following terms: *syntax*, *constituent*
1. Identify the major brain regions involved in processing sentences
1. Be able to locate Broca's area and Wernicke's area on an image of the brain
1. Explain the difference between *functional specialization* and *functional specificity*
1. Describe the evidence for this claim: Broca's region is not dedicated exclusively to language processing
1. Name one area of current controversy

:::

| Date | Topic | Reading | Resources |
| -- | -- | -- | -- | -- |
| Tues 10/6 | Sentences | SGN Ch. 9: *Building dependencies* | <span class="blue-badge">Slides ↗</span> | 
| Thurs 10/8 | Sentences | | 
| Tues 10/13 | Sentences | Matchin, Sprouse, & Hickok (2014)
| Thurs 10/15 | Aphasia | Dronkers & Baldo (2009)



<!-- Mentimeter>


<!-- Skip 
- SGN Ch. 7: *Structure and prediction* 
- SGN Ch. 8: *Composing sentences*
- Kim & Osterhout (2005)
- Bemis & Pylkkanen (2011?)
-->
